methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series F → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.3x) to $5.4B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 2 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Helion is a Series F climate and energy company founded by David Kirtley and Chris Pihl that has raised over $1B and carries a current post-money valuation of $15.5B. The valuation universe comprises 9 comparable companies in the Climate & Energy sector: 1 public (LNZA) and 8 private peers including Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, Electric Hydrogen, and Enovix.

Stage-progression analysis is the primary applicable method. Using Helion's last post-money valuation of $5.4B as the base and applying a typical Series F to next-stage step-up multiplier of 1.3x yields an implied valuation of $7.0B. Peer-median triangulation proved inapplicable due to an insufficient narrow peer set; while 8 same-sector comparables exist, fewer than 3 peers matched same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier filters simultaneously, making a focused median calculation unreliable. A broader sector median would introduce unacceptable noise.

The single-method triangulation supports a valuation range of $6.0B to $8.1B (median $7.0B), representing a 55–48% discount to the current $15.5B valuation. The material gap between this analytically derived range and current post-money reflects significant model limitation: the stage-progression multiplier alone cannot account for company-specific execution risk, market timing, or covenant structures embedded in late-stage financing. Key uncertainties include technology readiness validation and the durability of capital deployment pace relative to sector benchmarks.
